methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Contec is a publicly listed Space & Aerospace company on KOSDAQ that has raised $58.7M to date. The valuation universe consists of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though no narrow same-stage peer cohort exists within the sector.

Traditional triangulation methods face material constraints. Peer-Median analysis is not applicable: while 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, filtering for identical or adjacent stage and comparable valuation tier yields fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because Contec's revenue is not on file—a common limitation for private company valuation. The public Space & Aerospace sector shows a median price-to-sales multiple of 25.8x across the 6-ticker universe, but without Contec's disclosed revenue, this multiple cannot be directly applied.

The absence of tight peer filtration and missing revenue disclosure prevent derivation of a supported valuation range through standard triangulation. Any valuation estimate would require either disclosure of Contec's recent revenue or use of alternative methods (discounted cash flow, cost-to-replace) not addressed in available data. The $58.7M raised provides a floor but does not independently establish enterprise value. Further diligence on revenue scale and profitability trajectory is required before a defensible valuation range can be established.
